atoms.semantic_claim: The old pipeline setup accepts an optional canonical-state blocking handler, passes it from networked pipeline construction into the base pipeline builder, and installs it on the execution stage so blocking transaction processing runs during execution-stage canonical updates.

destinations.evidence: Current engine launch does not construct create_backfill_blocking_handler, does not pass a transaction-pool backfill handler into build_networked_pipeline, and exact searches found no process_canonical_state_transactions callback in this launch path.
Current pipeline setup has no blocking_handler parameter and no ExecutionStage::with_blocking_handler call, so the old execution-stage synchronous registration path is absent.

The replacement work now includes both provider.subscribe_blocking_canon_state for ordering-sensitive Ritual async lifecycle side effects and the existing asynchronous txpool maintenance stream for ordinary pool updates.

The maintenance task still handles Commit/Reorg notifications and calls pool.on_canonical_state_change(update), while TransactionPoolExt::on_blocking_canonical_state_change gives the provider-level blocking subscriber a pre-notification hook for Ritual async lifecycle state.

Pool::process_blocking_canonical_state_change now processes async commitments, async settlements, and expiry cleanup from the canonical tip. Pool::on_canonical_state_change delegates to the same idempotent hook after ordinary pool updates, preserving eventual stream maintenance and pre-notification async lifecycle visibility.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Resolved with v2-style structural equivalence. The port still does not thread an execution-stage blocking_handler through setup.rs, but the transaction-pool maintenance builder now registers its canonical async lifecycle hook with provider.subscribe_blocking_canon_state. The provider guarantees blocking handlers run before async canonical notifications, so the old ordering invariant is restored at the v2 ownership boundary.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Keep the txpool regression that proves a canonical async commitment processed through the blocking hook populates async metadata and sender locks before ordinary stream maintenance.
atoms.notes: Reviewed and updated 2026-05-11: setup.rs still lacks old execution-stage plumbing by design, but equivalent ordering is restored through provider.subscribe_blocking_canon_state in spawn_pool_maintenance_task.

summary: Reviewed and resolved: pipeline setup still does not thread the old execution-stage blocking handler, but the ordering-sensitive behavior is structurally covered by provider-level blocking canonical-state subscription from txpool maintenance.
notes: setup.rs has no blocking_handler parameter and no ExecutionStage::with_blocking_handler call; that old plumbing remains intentionally absent.
Pool maintenance now registers TransactionPoolExt::on_blocking_canonical_state_change with provider.subscribe_blocking_canon_state.
The provider-level blocking subscriber restores the old ordering guarantee before async canonical notifications.
The focused txpool regression and local HTTP async traffic passed after this wiring.
